name: Syrian Arabic (Damascene)
notes: Syrian Arabic — Damascene (شامي) urban koine, the most influential prestige form of Levantine. ق qaf → /ʔ/ glottal stop in the Damascus koine (rural/Bedouin /ɡ/ retained via the Levantine parent's variant list). ج jim → /ʒ/. Interdentals merged to stops in urban speech (θ→/t/, ð→/d/, ðˤ/ض→/dˤ/), conserved by rural/Druze speakers. Moderate imāla raises /aː/ → [eː] near front consonants. Source: Cowell (1964) A Reference Grammar of Syrian Arabic; Holes (2004).

sources.author: Cowell, M.W.
sources.title: A Reference Grammar of Syrian Arabic
sources.notes: Standard reference grammar; describes Damascus qaf→ʔ, jim→ʒ, interdental merger.

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
